Title: Add an allergy with allergen, reaction and severity
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient record open on the patient dashboard Allergies widget
Test_Steps: 1. Open the Allergies widget | 2. Click Add New Allergy | 3. Select allergen from coded list | 4. Select reaction | 5. Select severity | 6. Click Save
Expected_Result: Allergy is saved and displayed in the Allergies list with allergen, reaction and severity; confirmation message shown

Title: Add a drug allergy selecting allergen from drug catalog
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient record open; drug allergen catalog configured
Test_Steps: 1. Open Allergies widget | 2. Click Add New Allergy | 3. Choose category Drug | 4. Search and select drug allergen | 5. Add reaction and severity | 6. Save
Expected_Result: Drug allergy persisted with correct category; appears in allergy list with severe flag

Title: Add a food allergy
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient record open
Test_Steps: 1. Open Allergies widget | 2. Add New Allergy | 3. Choose category Food | 4. Select allergen Peanut | 5. Select reaction and severity | 6. Save
Expected_Result: Food allergy saved with correct category and displayed in list

Title: Add an environmental allergy
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient record open
Test_Steps: 1. Open Allergies widget | 2. Add New Allergy | 3. Choose category Environment | 4. Select allergen Latex | 5. Select reaction | 6. Save
Expected_Result: Environmental allergy saved and displayed correctly

Title: Add allergy with multiple reactions
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient record open
Test_Steps: 1. Open Allergies widget | 2. Add New Allergy | 3. Select allergen | 4. Add first reaction | 5. Add second reaction | 6. Set severity | 7. Save
Expected_Result: Allergy saved with both reactions listed against the single allergen

Title: Add allergy with onset date and comment note
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient record open
Test_Steps: 1. Open Allergies widget | 2. Add New Allergy | 3. Select allergen | 4. Enter onset date | 5. Enter free-text comment | 6. Save
Expected_Result: Allergy saved with onset date and comment persisted and viewable in detail

Title: Edit an existing allergy reaction and severity
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has at least one recorded allergy
Test_Steps: 1. Open Allergies widget | 2. Select existing allergy | 3. Click Edit | 4. Change reaction | 5. Change severity | 6. Save
Test_Data: Original Severity=Mild; New Severity=Severe; New Reaction=Anaphylaxis
Expected_Result: Allergy updated; list reflects new reaction and severity; prior values replaced

Title: Remove (void) an allergy with a void reason
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has a recorded allergy
Test_Steps: 1. Open Allergies widget | 2. Select allergy | 3. Click Delete/Remove | 4. Enter void reason | 5. Confirm
Expected_Result: Allergy is removed from active list and no longer displayed; void reason captured in audit

Title: Mark patient as No Known Allergies
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has no allergies recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Open Allergies widget | 2. Click No Known Allergies | 3. Confirm
Expected_Result: Allergies widget shows No Known Allergies status; Add Allergy still available to override

Title: Prevent No Known Allergies when an allergy already exists
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient already has one recorded allergy
Test_Steps: 1. Open Allergies widget | 2. Attempt to select No Known Allergies
Expected_Result: System blocks or warns that NKA cannot be set while active allergies exist; data remains consistent

Title: Add allergy after NKA flips status from No Known Allergies
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient currently marked No Known Allergies
Test_Steps: 1. Open Allergies widget showing NKA | 2. Click Add New Allergy | 3. Add allergen, reaction, severity | 4. Save
Expected_Result: NKA status is cleared and replaced by the new active allergy entry

Title: Prevent duplicate allergy for same allergen
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient already has Penicillin allergy recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Open Allergies widget | 2. Add New Allergy | 3. Select same allergen Penicillin | 4. Attempt Save
Expected_Result: System warns of duplicate allergen and prevents creating a second identical active allergy

Title: Reject saving allergy with no allergen selected
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient record open
Test_Steps: 1. Open Allergies widget | 2. Add New Allergy | 3. Leave allergen blank | 4. Select reaction and severity | 5. Click Save
Expected_Result: Save is blocked; validation message requires an allergen before saving

Title: Save allergy with allergen only and no reaction
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient record open
Test_Steps: 1. Open Allergies widget | 2. Add New Allergy | 3. Select allergen only | 4. Leave reaction and severity blank | 5. Save
Expected_Result: Allergy saved with allergen only if reaction is optional; reaction/severity shown as not specified

Title: Add Other (non-coded) allergen with free-text name
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient record open
Test_Steps: 1. Open Allergies widget | 2. Add New Allergy | 3. Select Other allergen | 4. Enter free-text allergen name | 5. Add reaction and severity | 6. Save
Expected_Result: Non-coded allergen saved with free-text name and displayed in list

Title: Reject allergen free-text exceeding max length
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient record open
Test_Steps: 1. Open Allergies widget | 2. Add New Allergy | 3. Select Other | 4. Enter 256-character allergen text | 5. Save
Expected_Result: System rejects or truncates per field limit; clear validation message shown

Title: Decision table for severity-driven alert behavior
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; clinical decision support for allergy alerts enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Add allergy with Mild severity and verify alert | 2. Add allergy with Moderate severity and verify alert | 3. Add allergy with Severe severity and verify alert | 4. Order a drug matching a Severe allergen
Test_Data: Severities=Mild,Moderate,Severe; Drug order matching Severe allergen
Expected_Result: Mild/Moderate produce informational note; Severe triggers a hard interaction alert on matching drug order per decision rules

Title: Pairwise coverage of category x severity x reaction
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient record open
Test_Steps: 1. Create allergies covering pairwise combinations of category, severity and reaction | 2. Verify each saves correctly | 3. Verify list display
Expected_Result: All pairwise combinations save and display correctly without category-specific failures

Title: Exploratory testing of allergy widget rapid edits
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient with several allergies
Test_Steps: 1. Rapidly add, edit and remove allergies | 2. Switch categories mid-entry | 3. Cancel and re-open form | 4. Observe state consistency
Expected_Result: No orphaned records, stale UI state, or lost data; widget remains consistent after rapid interactions

Title: Allergy form is keyboard navigable and screen-reader labeled
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; assistive technology available; allergy form open
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate allergy form using Tab only | 2. Verify focus order | 3. Verify each field has a programmatic label | 4. Submit using Enter
Expected_Result: All controls reachable via keyboard, focus visible, fields announced with labels per WCAG 2.1 AA

Title: Clinician role required to modify allergies
Preconditions: Two users: one Clinician with edit rights, one Receptionist without
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as Receptionist | 2. Open patient allergies | 3. Attempt to add or edit an allergy
Expected_Result: Add/Edit controls hidden or blocked; unauthorized modification denied and logged

Title: Audit log records allergy creation with user and timestamp
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; DB/audit access available to verifier
Test_Steps: 1. Add a new allergy | 2. Query the audit/obs records | 3. Verify creator, datetime and value persisted
Expected_Result: Audit entry exists with creating user, UTC timestamp and recorded allergen value

Title: Create allergy via REST API returns 201 with resource
Preconditions: Valid session/credentials; patient UUID known; REST API reachable
Test_Steps: 1. POST to /ws/rest/v1/patient/{uuid}/allergy with allergen, reaction, severity | 2. Inspect response | 3. GET to confirm persistence
Test_Data: allergen coded ref; reaction coded ref; severity=SEVERE
Expected_Result: API returns 201 with new allergy UUID; subsequent GET returns the created allergy

Title: Reject allergy creation via API with invalid severity code
Preconditions: Valid session/credentials; patient UUID known
Test_Steps: 1. POST allergy with invalid severity value | 2. Inspect response code and body
Expected_Result: API returns 400/422 with validation error; no allergy created

Title: Export allergy as FHIR AllergyIntolerance resource
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; patient has a recorded allergy
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/AllergyIntolerance?patient={id} | 2. Validate resource structure | 3. Verify code, reaction.manifestation and criticality
Expected_Result: Returns valid FHIR R4 AllergyIntolerance with clinicalStatus, code, criticality and reaction.manifestation correctly mapped

Title: FHIR AllergyIntolerance uses correct coding system URIs
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; SNOMED/RxNorm mappings configured
Test_Steps: 1. GET AllergyIntolerance resource | 2. Inspect code.coding.system values | 3. Validate against expected system URIs
Expected_Result: code.coding.system uses correct canonical URIs; codes resolve to valid concepts

Title: FHIR criticality maps from internal severity correctly
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; allergies with varying severities exist
Test_Steps: 1. Create allergies of Mild, Moderate, Severe | 2. GET each as FHIR | 3. Verify criticality mapping
Expected_Result: Internal severity maps to FHIR criticality (low/high) per defined mapping rules

Title: Add an active condition with onset date
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient record open; Conditions widget available
Test_Steps: 1. Open Conditions widget | 2. Click Add Condition | 3. Search and select coded condition | 4. Set status Active | 5. Enter onset date | 6. Save
Expected_Result: Active condition saved and displayed in active conditions list with onset date

Title: Add a coded condition selected from concept dictionary
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient record open; concept dictionary loaded
Test_Steps: 1. Open Conditions widget | 2. Add Condition | 3. Type to search condition | 4. Select coded result | 5. Save
Expected_Result: Condition saved with coded concept reference, not free text; appears in list

Title: Add a non-coded condition as free text
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient record open; free-text conditions allowed
Test_Steps: 1. Open Conditions widget | 2. Add Condition | 3. Enter free-text condition name | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Non-coded condition saved as free text and flagged as non-coded in the list

Title: Edit condition onset date and status
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active condition
Test_Steps: 1. Open Conditions widget | 2. Select condition | 3. Edit | 4. Change onset date | 5. Save
Expected_Result: Condition updated with new onset date; change reflected in list and history

Title: Transition condition from Active to Inactive
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active condition
Test_Steps: 1. Open Conditions widget | 2. Select active condition | 3. Change status to Inactive | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Condition moves to inactive list; no longer shown as active; status history retained

Title: Transition condition from Inactive to Resolved with end date
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an inactive condition
Test_Steps: 1. Open Conditions widget | 2. Select inactive condition | 3. Change status to Resolved | 4. Enter end/resolution date | 5. Save
Expected_Result: Condition marked Resolved with end date; moved to resolved/history view

Title: Full lifecycle Active to Inactive to Resolved
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; new active condition created
Test_Steps: 1. Add active condition | 2. Change to Inactive | 3. Change to Resolved with end date | 4. Verify history at each step
Expected_Result: Each transition persists; history shows ordered Active->Inactive->Resolved with dates

Title: Reactivate a resolved condition back to active
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has a resolved condition
Test_Steps: 1. Open Conditions widget | 2. Select resolved condition | 3. Change status to Active | 4. Clear end date | 5. Save
Expected_Result: Condition returns to active list; end date cleared; status history records reactivation

Title: Reject condition onset date in the future
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient record open
Test_Steps: 1. Open Conditions widget | 2. Add Condition | 3. Select condition | 4. Enter future onset date | 5. Save
Expected_Result: Save blocked; validation message that onset date cannot be in the future

Title: Reject end date earlier than onset date
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has condition with onset date
Test_Steps: 1. Edit condition | 2. Set status Resolved | 3. Enter end date before onset | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Save blocked; validation that resolution date must be on or after onset date

Title: Onset date boundary equal to today is accepted
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient record open
Test_Steps: 1. Add condition | 2. Enter onset date equal to current date | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Condition saved successfully with today as onset date; no validation error

Title: Prevent duplicate active condition for same coded concept
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient already has active Hypertension
Test_Steps: 1. Open Conditions widget | 2. Add Condition | 3. Select Hypertension again as Active | 4. Save
Expected_Result: System warns of duplicate active condition and prevents or flags the redundant entry

Title: Void a condition with a reason
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has a condition
Test_Steps: 1. Open Conditions widget | 2. Select condition | 3. Delete/Void | 4. Enter void reason | 5. Confirm
Expected_Result: Condition removed from list; void reason stored for audit

Title: Decision table for condition status x date field requirements
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient record open
Test_Steps: 1. Active requires onset, no end date | 2. Inactive optional end date | 3. Resolved requires end date | 4. Verify field requirements per status
Expected_Result: Required/optional date fields enforced per status rule combination; mismatches rejected

Title: Export condition as FHIR Condition resource
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; patient has a coded condition
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Condition?patient={id} | 2. Validate clinicalStatus and code | 3. Verify onsetDateTime
Expected_Result: Returns valid FHIR Condition with clinicalStatus active, coded code and onsetDateTime mapped

Title: FHIR Condition clinicalStatus reflects resolved state
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; patient has a resolved condition
Test_Steps: 1. Resolve a condition | 2. GET FHIR Condition | 3. Verify clinicalStatus=resolved and abatementDateTime present
Expected_Result: FHIR clinicalStatus=resolved and abatementDateTime mapped from resolution date

Title: Retrieve patient conditions via REST API
Preconditions: Valid session; patient UUID with conditions; REST reachable
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/rest/v1/condition?patientUuid={uuid} | 2. Inspect response array | 3. Verify status and concept fields
Expected_Result: API returns conditions with correct status, concept and onset for the patient

Title: Add a primary diagnosis at an encounter
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; active visit/encounter open; Visit Diagnoses widget available
Test_Steps: 1. Open Diagnoses widget within encounter | 2. Search and select coded diagnosis | 3. Set order Primary | 4. Set certainty Confirmed | 5. Save
Expected_Result: Primary diagnosis recorded against the encounter and shown in diagnosis list

Title: Add a secondary diagnosis to the same encounter
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; encounter already has a primary diagnosis
Test_Steps: 1. Open Diagnoses widget | 2. Add another diagnosis | 3. Set order Secondary | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Secondary diagnosis added alongside primary; both display with correct order

Title: Add a presumed (provisional) diagnosis
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; encounter open
Test_Steps: 1. Open Diagnoses widget | 2. Select diagnosis | 3. Set certainty Presumed | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Diagnosis saved with presumed certainty and distinguishable from confirmed in list

Title: Add diagnosis coded with ICD-10
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; ICD-10 mappings configured on concepts
Test_Steps: 1. Open Diagnoses widget | 2. Search diagnosis | 3. Select concept with ICD-10 mapping | 4. Save | 5. Verify ICD-10 code
Expected_Result: Diagnosis stored with mapped ICD-10 code J18.9 available for reporting/billing

Title: Add diagnosis coded with SNOMED CT
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; SNOMED mappings configured
Test_Steps: 1. Open Diagnoses widget | 2. Search diagnosis | 3. Select SNOMED-mapped concept | 4. Save | 5. Verify SNOMED code
Expected_Result: Diagnosis stored with SNOMED CT code 233604007 and correct system URI

Title: Reject diagnosis with no clinical code mapping in coded-only mode
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; system configured to require coded diagnoses
Test_Steps: 1. Open Diagnoses widget | 2. Attempt to enter free-text uncoded diagnosis | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Save blocked; system requires a coded diagnosis when coded-only mode is enforced

Title: Prevent two primary diagnoses on one encounter
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; encounter already has a primary diagnosis
Test_Steps: 1. Open Diagnoses widget | 2. Add second diagnosis | 3. Set order Primary | 4. Save
Expected_Result: System prevents a second primary or auto-demotes prior primary per rule; only one primary remains

Title: Prevent duplicate diagnosis on same encounter
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; encounter has Pneumonia diagnosis
Test_Steps: 1. Open Diagnoses widget | 2. Add Pneumonia again | 3. Save
Expected_Result: System warns of duplicate diagnosis on the encounter and prevents redundant entry

Title: Change diagnosis order from secondary to primary
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; encounter has a secondary diagnosis and no primary
Test_Steps: 1. Open Diagnoses widget | 2. Edit secondary diagnosis | 3. Change order to Primary | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Diagnosis order updated to Primary; list reflects new ordering

Title: Remove a diagnosis from an encounter with reason
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; encounter has a diagnosis
Test_Steps: 1. Open Diagnoses widget | 2. Select diagnosis | 3. Remove/Void | 4. Enter reason | 5. Confirm
Expected_Result: Diagnosis removed from encounter; void reason stored for audit

Title: Decision table for order x certainty combinations
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; encounter open
Test_Steps: 1. Primary-Confirmed | 2. Primary-Presumed | 3. Secondary-Confirmed | 4. Secondary-Presumed | 5. Verify each saves and displays correctly
Expected_Result: All valid order/certainty combinations save and render with correct labels and reporting flags

Title: Pairwise coverage of coding system x order x certainty
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; ICD-10 and SNOMED mappings configured
Test_Steps: 1. Create diagnoses across pairwise combinations of coding system, order and certainty | 2. Verify save and code retention
Expected_Result: All pairwise combinations persist with the correct code and attributes

Title: Export diagnosis as FHIR Condition with encounter context
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; encounter diagnosis exists
Test_Steps: 1. GET FHIR Condition for diagnosis | 2. Verify category=encounter-diagnosis | 3. Verify code and encounter reference
Expected_Result: FHIR Condition returned with encounter-diagnosis category, coded code and encounter reference

Title: FHIR diagnosis rank/use reflects primary vs secondary
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; encounter has primary and secondary diagnoses
Test_Steps: 1. GET FHIR Encounter or Condition | 2. Verify diagnosis.use or rank reflects primary/secondary
Expected_Result: Diagnosis ranking exposed via FHIR distinguishes primary from secondary correctly

Title: Diagnosis exported in HL7 v2 DG1 segment
Preconditions: HL7 v2 outbound interface configured (platform interface engine)
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger ADT/charge message containing diagnosis | 2. Capture HL7 message | 3. Inspect DG1 segment fields
Expected_Result: HL7 message contains DG1 with correct diagnosis code, coding system and primary/secondary indicator

Title: Allergy exported in HL7 v2 AL1 segment
Preconditions: HL7 v2 outbound interface configured (platform interface engine)
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger ADT message for a patient with allergy | 2. Capture HL7 message | 3. Inspect AL1 segment
Expected_Result: HL7 message includes AL1 segment with allergen, type and severity mapped correctly

Title: Allergy and diagnosis access restricted to authorized roles via API
Preconditions: API reachable; a low-privilege service account without clinical read rights
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate as low-privilege account | 2. GET allergy and diagnosis endpoints | 3. Inspect response
Expected_Result: API returns 401/403; no PHI exposed to unauthorized caller; attempt logged

Title: Database audit trail captures status transitions for conditions
Preconditions: DB access available; condition transitioned through states
Test_Steps: 1. Transition condition Active->Inactive->Resolved | 2. Query audit/history tables | 3. Verify each transition row
Expected_Result: Each status change recorded with previous value, new value, user and timestamp

Title: Reject script payload in free-text condition/allergy comment
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient record open
Test_Steps: 1. Add condition with script payload in comment | 2. Save | 3. Reopen record and view comment
Expected_Result: Input is sanitized/encoded; no script executes; stored value rendered safely

Title: Clinical coding validation rejects invalid ICD-10 code format
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; coded diagnosis entry; coding validation enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to enter a malformed ICD-10 code | 2. Save
Expected_Result: System rejects invalid code format; validation message shown; no diagnosis saved

Title: Allergy round-trips through Resource Adapter Layer across platforms
Preconditions: Resource Adapter Layer configured for OpenMRS, OpenEMR, HAPI FHIR and omiiCARE
Test_Steps: 1. Create allergy via adapter on OpenMRS | 2. Read via adapter on HAPI FHIR mapping | 3. Compare normalized fields
Expected_Result: Adapter normalizes allergen, reaction and severity consistently across target systems with no data loss

Title: Exploratory check of allergy alert at drug order time
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has a Severe drug allergy; order entry available
Test_Steps: 1. Order a drug matching the recorded allergen | 2. Observe alert | 3. Attempt override with reason | 4. Order an unrelated drug
Expected_Result: Interaction alert fires for matching allergen, override requires reason; unrelated drug produces no alert

Title: Conditions list color/status not conveyed by color alone
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; conditions in multiple statuses present
Test_Steps: 1. View conditions list | 2. Inspect status indicators | 3. Verify text/icon accompanies color | 4. Check contrast
Expected_Result: Status conveyed by text/icon in addition to color; contrast meets WCAG 2.1 AA

Title: Add maximum supported number of diagnoses to an encounter
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; encounter open
Test_Steps: 1. Add diagnoses up to the configured maximum | 2. Attempt to add one beyond the limit
Test_Data: Add N max diagnoses then N+1
Expected_Result: All up to the limit save; the over-limit attempt is rejected with a clear message
